meta.subject: Life Sciences
notation: Use the 'therefore' symbol (∴) before final answers where applicable
Use decimal point (not comma) for decimals
Diagrams must be drawn with pencil and labelled clearly
markingStandards: Full marks require correct scientific terminology, clear diagrams with labels and headings, and all steps shown where required
Method marks for correct approach and reasoning
Diagrams assessed for accuracy, proportion, labels and captions
Consistent accuracy marks apply across related questions

terminology.note: Give a detailed account in words or diagrams
Give reasons for a phenomenon or process
Present information in a table format

meta.assessmentFormat: Two examination papers: Paper 1 focuses on Knowledge Strands 1 and 2 (Life at the molecular, cellular and tissue level; Life processes in plants and animals). Paper 2 focuses on Knowledge Strands 3 and 4 (Environmental studies; Diversity, change and continuity). Practical tasks and investigations are also assessed.

subtopics.name: Life Sciences Orientation
skills: Understand the nature and scope of Life Sciences
Explain the scientific method and its application in Life Sciences
Identify important principles and relationships in Life Sciences
Recognise the value of indigenous knowledge systems in Life Sciences
methods: Apply the steps of the scientific method (observation, hypothesis, experiment, conclusion)
Distinguish between qualitative and quantitative data
commonSteps: Design and conduct a simple investigation

topics.topic: Life at the Molecular, Cellular and Tissue Level

subtopics.name: The Chemistry of Life
skills: Distinguish between inorganic and organic compounds important for life
Describe the role and functions of water, minerals and vitamins
Explain the structure and functions of organic compounds: carbohydrates, lipids, proteins and nucleic acids
Discuss deficiency diseases related to nutrients and the importance of a balanced diet
methods: Analyse nutritional information on food packaging
Link chemical properties to biological functions
commonSteps: Describe its building blocks and structure
Explain its role in living organisms

subtopics.name: Cells: The Basic Unit of Life
skills: Compare the structure of plant and animal cells
Describe the structure and functions of cell organelles
Explain the role of the cell membrane in movement of substances (diffusion, osmosis, active transport)
Discuss cell differentiation and specialisation
methods: Use microscope or micrographs to observe cells
Draw and label plant and animal cells accurately

subtopics.name: Cell Division: Mitosis
skills: Describe the process of mitosis and its importance
Identify and describe the stages of mitosis
Explain the role of mitosis in growth, repair and asexual reproduction
Discuss the uncontrolled division of cells (cancer)
methods: Observe prepared slides or diagrams of mitotic cells
commonSteps: Identify the stage of mitosis from diagrams or slides

subtopics.name: Plant and Animal Tissues
skills: Describe the structure and functions of plant tissues (meristematic, epidermal, parenchyma, collenchyma, sclerenchyma, xylem, phloem)
Describe the structure and functions of animal tissues (epithelial, connective, muscle, nervous)
Explain how tissues are organised into organs
methods: Observe and draw tissues using microscope or micrographs
commonSteps: Classify tissues as simple or complex
Link cell structure to tissue function

topics.topic: Life Processes in Plants and Animals

subtopics.name: Support and Transport Systems in Plants
skills: Describe the anatomy of dicotyledonous root and stem
Explain secondary growth and the formation of annual rings
Describe the uptake and transport of water and minerals
Explain transpiration and factors affecting it
methods: Draw and label cross-sections of root and stem

subtopics.name: Support Systems in Animals
skills: Compare the human skeleton with endoskeletons and exoskeletons of other animals
Describe the structure and functions of the human skeletal system
Explain joint types and their role in movement
Discuss locomotion in animals

subtopics.name: Transport System in Mammals (Humans)
skills: Describe the structure and functions of the human heart and blood vessels
Explain the cardiac cycle and blood circulation (systemic and pulmonary)
Describe the composition and functions of blood
Discuss cardiovascular diseases and their prevention
methods: Draw and label the heart and blood circulation diagrams

subtopics.name: Biosphere to Ecosystems
skills: Define the biosphere, biomes and ecosystems
Describe the abiotic and biotic components of an ecosystem
Explain energy flow through food chains and food webs
Describe nutrient cycles (water, carbon, nitrogen)
Discuss the importance of biodiversity and threats to ecosystems
methods: Construct food chains and food webs
commonSteps: Explain cycling of matter and flow of energy

subtopics.name: Biodiversity and Classification
skills: Explain the concept of biodiversity
Describe the five-kingdom classification system
Classify organisms according to observable features
Discuss the importance of classification and binomial nomenclature

subtopics.name: History of Life on Earth
skills: Describe key events in the history of life on Earth
Explain evidence for evolution (fossils, biogeography, comparative anatomy)
Discuss mass extinctions and their causes
Interpret geological time scale
